race.comment: The outside barrier has presented a challenge to ARK SEA (12) but she came from there to easily win her heat last week. There is obviously more depth in this final but her campaign has been timed to have her peaking tonight and class will carry her a long way. The timing also looks right for ARRHYTHMIA (9). She set the benchmark for juvenile fillies in Victoria last season by winning both the Vicbred and Breeders Crown finals last season and came from wider in winning her heat last week. She has won both previous third-up attempts so will be peaking. Massive danger. SPARKLING SEA (11) got back on track with her Golden Easter Egg win at this circuit and distance last start. Draws much wider but came from this barrier when she won the Raith Memorial here in March. WILLOWS GIRL (5) is impossible to knock having won all four starts this time in, highlighted by her win over several of these rivals in the Gold Bracelet. Must go in along with RIPPLES NZ (10) who draws wide but is honest.
